methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Mission Barns, a Series B biotech company founded by Eitan Fischer and David Bowman, has raised $50M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 8 private and 3 public companies in the Biotech & Health sector, with primary focus on 5 same-stage private peers: Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M).

Two methods prove applicable. Stage-Progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $50M raised proxy, implying a $95M valuation. Peer-Median analysis across the 5 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $810M, with a range from $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to lack of Mission Barns revenue disclosure.

The triangulated analysis across these two methods supports a valuation range of $81M–$931M, with a median of $452M. The Stage-Progression method anchors the lower bound; the Peer-Median method dominates the upper bound. Key uncertainties include the wide spread within the peer cohort (5.5x between min and max), which reflects heterogeneity in biotech valuation drivers not observable from valuation data alone, and Mission Barns' unreported revenue, which prevents calibration via public-market multiples.
